channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump revels in mass federal firings and jeers at Biden before adoring conservative crowd
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Saturday that “nobody's ever seen anything" like his administration's sweeping effort to fire thousands of federal employees and shrink the size of government, congratulating himself for “dominating” Washington and sending bureaucrats “packing.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Coal still key to US energy dominance, says West Virginia governor
item.description: West Virginia Republican Gov. Patrick Morrisey touted the Mountain State's coal infrastructure as the unsung key to battling China in energy dominance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at Dan 'Razin' Caine, Trump's pick to be the top US military officer
item.description: President Donald Trump’s choice to be the top U.S. military officer, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine, endeared himself to the commander in chief through his military call sign, “Razin,” during a meeting in Iraq years ago.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: CPAC straw poll reveals who conservatives believe will be 2028 presidential nominee
item.description: Vice President JD Vance will be the GOP's presidential nominee in the 2028 election cycle, a straw poll conducted at the 2025 Conservative Political Action Conference found.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know as Michigan Republicans select new chair to lead party into the 2026 midterms
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Michigan Republicans are gathering in Detroit on Saturday to elect a new chair who will lead the party into the 2026 midterms in the battleground state, when several key races will be decided including a critical U.S. Senate seat.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nonprofit working on Mississippi River project loses federal funding
item.description: Mississippi Park Connection said funds for restoration work that has not yet been completed are frozen, as are funds for congressionally funded work that has already been completed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump moves with light speed and brute force in shaking the core of what America has been
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is moving with light speed and brute force to break the existing order and reshape America at home and abroad. He likes the ring of calling himself king. No one can absorb it all.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US offers UN resolution on war in Ukraine that's far short of rival European statement
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States has proposed a draft U.N. resolution that stops far short of a competing European-backed statement demanding an immediate withdrawal of all of Moscow’s forces from Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC sues after Trump admin claws back $80M migrant housing grant
item.description: The Eric Adams administration filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration on Friday seeking to claw back more than $80 million previously sent to the city to house migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New FBI Director Kash Patel plans to move up to 1,500 workers out of Washington, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New FBI Director Kash Patel has told senior officials that he plans to relocate up to 1,000 employees from Washington to field offices around the country and move an additional 500 to a large bureau facility in Huntsville, Alabama, according to a person with knowledge of the discus
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. Department of Agriculture suspends scholarship program at NC A&T State University, 18 other historically Black colleges, website shows
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (WGHP) — The U.S. Department of Agriculture has suspended a scholarship program that serves students from rural or underserved communities pending further review, according to its website.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, NewsNation

item.title: Union workers written up, fired for using sick days; Nevada lawmaker moves to close loophole
item.description: Some union workers are afraid to use sick days because they might be written up or fired for being "undependable," and now a Nevada lawmaker is coming to their aid.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dexter's Law: Pushing for tougher sentencing for animal cruelty
item.description: One Tampa Bay area lawmaker is pushing to toughen sentences for animal cruelty, specifically in cases like the shocking discovery of Dexter, a decapitated dog found at Fort DeSoto Beach.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Frey vetoes council move toward a pedestrian plaza at George Floyd Square
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed the City Council’s plan to explore building a pedestrian-only mall at George Floyd Square, the intersection where an officer killed Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: Fellow senators have clapped for Mitch McConnell after he announced he won’t seek reelection next year. The Kentucky Republican is ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Black farmers among those left in limbo amid federal funding freeze
item.description: The federal government has historically discriminated against Black farmers. The recent funding halt from the Trump administration presents yet another hurdle to those who have spent decades fighting for equity in farming.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Peñaloza, MPR News

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay sheriffs to advise Florida cabinet on immigration enforcement
item.description: Two Tampa Bay area sheriffs were tapped to join the newly created State Immigration Enforcement Council on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: New Florida Attorney General sworn in, first priority illegal immigration
item.description: Florida’s new Attorney General has been sworn in and he's hitting the ground, running. After taking the oath of office, James Uthmeier stood with Gov. Ron DeSantis and the cabinet holding an emergency meeting to implement immigration enforcement measures.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5
